behavior_summary: U8P_HAMMER::look is the item's 'U8P_HAMMER::look' (examine) handler. When the player inspects the HAMMER it displays a single short bark reading 'hammer ' and then suspends/ends the U8P_HAMMER::look process.
There is no branching, menu interaction, or flag reads/writes in this script — behavior is a one-shot descriptive display.
Item properties (e.g., baseDamage, damageModifier, damage type, treasureChance) are present in the file metadata but are not read or modified by U8P_HAMMER::look.
notable_control_flow: Straight-line sequence: call Bark('hammer ') then call suspend (inferred to end or pause the process).
No conditional logic, loops, or menu/choice handling are present.
open_questions: Does suspend here immediately terminate the U8P_HAMMER::look process or does it wait for a player keypress/close action? (suspend meaning is inferred from low-level node).
Are there additional processes (use, pickup, equip) for the HAMMER defined elsewhere that interact with the item's metadata?
Is the trailing space in the bark text 'hammer ' deliberate (formatting/concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?

main_function.name: U8P_HAMMER::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short on-screen description when the player examines the HAMMER and then suspend/close the U8P_HAMMER::look interaction.
entry_conditions: Player triggers the engine's U8P_HAMMER::look/inspect action on the HAMMER (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_HAMMER::look
function_map.role: examine/description handler for the HAMMER item

function_map.name: Bark
function_map.role: display short player-facing text (used to show 'hammer ')

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: suspend or terminate the current process (inferred usage to end the U8P_HAMMER::look event)

external_calls.purpose: Display the one-line description text 'hammer ' to the player.
Suspend or end the U8P_HAMMER::look process after the bark (inferred to finalize the examine interaction).
